column1: # Directors Dealings — static ticker -> sector/benchmark/AIM lookup.
#   * `sector` is a free-text FTSE-aligned label. The signal engine
#   * `benchmark_symbol` is the Yahoo `^...` symbol used to look up that
#     ticker's sector index in the `prices` table. All rows use `^FTAS`
#   * `is_aim`: 0/1. AIM flag is needed by Stage 4 for stamp-duty
#     exemption (AIM stocks pay 0% UK stamp duty; main market 0.5%).
#     The signal engine charges 50bps stamp duty on Main-Market buys
#     only — getting this wrong over-charges or under-charges spread.
#   Backtest revealed only 53 of 70 active tickers were classified
#   only 5 of the AIM names were marked. This pass added 65 tickers and
#   AIM classification sources used (in priority order):
#     1. Wikipedia individual company pages: phrases like "Alternative
#        positive signal; "FTSE 100/250/SmallCap" treated as Main signal.
#     2. Yahoo Finance ticker -> longName lookup (LSE exchange only).
#     3. Project knowledge for known AIM constituents (Jet2
#     4. Investment trusts default to Main Market (closed-end funds
#   Bias rule: when classification is uncertain
#   cost model over-charges 50bps which is safer than under-charging.
#   anonymous chart endpoint. Per "remove any symbol that 404s" rule
#   all rows use `^FTAS` (FTSE All-Share). A future stage can re-probe
#   ICB (^FTIICB*) or MSCI UK sector ETF proxies and upgrade in place.
# Manual update path: edit and re-run `fetch_sectors.py`.
